new_dialogue.text: That last iframe still doesn’t show up where I’m pasting it. Any quick checklist for why an embed might not render?

Common causes:
1) You’re pasting into a place that strips HTML (many chats/forums do).
2) The site uses a sanitizer that removes iframes unless whitelisted.
3) Mixed-content/CSP: page is HTTPS but iframe blocked by Content-Security-Policy.
4) Wrong URL format: needs https://www.youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID (not watch?v=...).
5) Referrer / X-Frame-Options restrictions (some hosts block embedding).
If you tell me where you’re pasting it (e.g., CMS editor, markdown, forum post), I can narrow it down.
It’s a markdown editor that sometimes supports HTML. How can I test if iframes are blocked without opening devtools?

Try a simple controlled test: - Paste: bold — if it renders as bold, HTML is allowed. - Paste a harmless iframe to a known-embeddable page; if it disappears entirely, it’s likely stripped by sanitization. - If it stays but shows a blank box, it’s more likely CSP/mixed-content. Also check if the platform offers a dedicated “Embed” block instead of raw HTML.
Ok. Separate question: why do some platforms allow images but block iframes?

Security and abuse risk. Images are passive; iframes can run third‑party scripts, track users, phish logins, or overlay deceptive UI. Blocking iframes reduces XSS-like vectors and limits what external content can do inside the page. Many platforms only allow a curated embed list (YouTube, Vimeo, etc.) via an embed widget to keep control.
